topic: ChatGPT prompting strategies and real-world use cases

entries.a: Prompts.chat is the world's largest open-source prompt library for AI, designed to work with various AI models including ChatGPT, Claude, and others.

entries.q: What types of prompts can be found on prompts.chat?
entries.a: The library contains a curated collection of prompt examples that can be used with AI chat models, originally created for ChatGPT but compatible with modern AI assistants.

entries.q: How can users contribute to prompts.chat?
entries.a: Users can contribute by adding prompts at prompts.chat/prompts/new, where submissions sync automatically with the library.

entries.q: What is the Interactive Book of Prompting?
entries.a: The Interactive Book of Prompting is a free, interactive guide that covers over 25 chapters on prompt engineering, including techniques like chain-of-thought reasoning and few-shot learning.

entries.q: What is the purpose of the 'Prompting for Kids' section?
entries.a: The 'Prompting for Kids' section offers an interactive, game-based adventure designed to teach children aged 8-14 how to communicate with AI through fun puzzles and stories.

entries.q: What options are available for self-hosting prompts.chat?
entries.a: Users can deploy their own private prompt library with custom branding and authentication through a quick start command or manual setup using Git.

entries.q: How can prompts.chat be integrated with other tools?
entries.a: Prompts.chat can be integrated as a CLI tool, a plugin for Claude, or used as an MCP server in AI tools, with specific configurations provided for each method.
